sentences.text: If you're not the one racking breakers: Most fire/life-safety work in an MCC or e-house is installing or servicing detection, notification, or suppression-release wiring routed near or through these enclosures — not switching the gear itself.
That doesn't reduce your exposure: cable troughs, junction boxes, and conduit runs inside or adjacent to an energized lineup still put you inside the arc flash boundary.

Confirm with the area electrician or operations whether the specific bucket/cabinet you need access to can be de-energized for your task, and never assume "I'm just running a wire" exempts you from the label's PPE requirement.
